channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: Trump Threatens Iranian Boats He Says Are Mining the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Oil prices hovered above $100 a barrel as reported ship seizures added to market anxiety. The White House said President Trump does not view Iran's reported seizures as a cease-fire violation.

item.title: Trump orders U.S. military to 'shoot and kill' Iranian boats mining Strait of Hormuz
item.description: US President Trump ordered the military to shoot and kill Iranian boats mining the Strait of Hormuz. Live updates: Trump orders U. S. military to 'shoot and kill' Iranian boats mining Strait of Hormuz NBC News.

item.title: Teenager becomes first Congo virus fatality in Pakistan's Karachi
item.description: A teenager in Karachi became the first Congo virus fatality in Pakistan. Concern among health officials in Karachi with the festival of Id al-Azha due next month. Teenager becomes first Congo virus fatality in Pakistan's Karachi.

item.title: New multilateral force for gang-plagued Haiti to deploy soon, UN told
item.description: A new multilateral force will deploy to Haiti to address gang issues. New multilateral force for gang-plagued Haiti to deploy soon, UN told France 24.

item.title: Above 92% turnout in Bengal, 85% in Tamil Nadu amid poll violence | Above the Fold | 23.04.2026
item.description: Bengal saw 92% voter turnout and Tamil Nadu 85% amid poll violence. In today’s episode, we look at the key election trends in Tamil Nadu and West Bengal, tracking major shifts ahead of polling.

item.title: Trump orders U.S. Navy to 'shoot, kill' small boats laying mines in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Pakistan's minister and a US envoy discussed efforts for a second round of US-Iran talks. Iran’s vow not to reopen the Strait of Hormuz so long as a U. S. naval blockade remained in place.

item.title: "Uninformed, Inappropriate, In Poor Taste": India After Trump 'Hell-Hole' Row
item.description: India criticized Donald Trump for sharing a derogatory post about the country. Donald Trump had shared a derogatory post on India made by a podcaster. "Uninformed, Inappropriate, In Poor Taste": India After Trump 'Hell-Hole' Row.

item.title: Trump tells BBC that King's visit could 'absolutely' help repair relations with UK
item.description: Donald Trump said the King's visit could help repair US and UK relations. In a phone interview with the BBC's North America editor, the president discussed next week's visit and his relationship with the UK PM.

item.title: Germany, a Steadfast Ally of Israel, Now Voices Some Frustration
item.description: German officials criticized Israel, conveying Berlin's frustration with its ally. In recent weeks, German officials have issued critical comments about Israel that convey Berlin’s recent frustration with its longtime ally.

item.title: Russian Town Sees Toxic Rain After Ukraine Strike, Officials Issue Warning
item.description: Ukraine's drone attack on a Russian oil refinery in Tuapse caused toxic rain. Thick plumes of smoke rise from an oil refinery in Russia's Tuapse after an Ukrainian drone attack.

item.title: US-Israel-Iran War Ceasefire News Live Updates: 'Waiting for US greenlight', Israel says ready to
item.description: Iran received its first revenue from tolls collected at the Strait of Hormuz. The payment was made after a recent agreement. The official did not disclose the revenue amount. US-Israel-Iran War Ceasefire News Live Updates: Iran receives first revenue from Strait of Hormuz tolls, says official The Times of India.

item.title: Trump issues lethal order after US seizes Iranian oil tanker in Indian Ocean
item.description: The US seized an Iranian oil tanker in the Indian Ocean, prompting a response. Trump issues lethal order after US seizes Iranian oil tanker in Indian Ocean.

item.title: US-Israeli war on Iran will push 30 million back into poverty, UN warns
item.description: The US-Israeli conflict with Iran may push 30 million into poverty, the UN warns. Disruption to fuel and fertiliser supplies due to the Strait of Hormuz closure will hit crop yields, UNDP chief warns.

item.title: Old-Economy Investments Gain New Appeal in Confounding Markets
item.description: Old-economy investments are gaining appeal due to market uncertainty. Old-Economy Investments Gain New Appeal in Confounding Markets Bloomberg. com.

item.title: Exiled Iranian crown prince splattered with red liquid in Berlin after slashing US-Iran truce
item.description: Iranian crown prince Reza Pahlavi was splattered with red liquid in Berlin after criticizing a US-Iran truce. The incident occurred outside a hotel where Pahlavi was speaking. Police are investigating the attack.

item.title: Warner Bros shareholders approve Paramount’s takeover
item.description: Warner Bros shareholders approved Paramount's takeover. The deal still requires regulatory approval from Washington and London. Ongoing reviews by the US Department of Justice are expected to examine the merger's impact.

item.title: Warner Bros Shareholders Approve Paramount's $81 Billion Takeover
item.description: Warner Bros shareholders approved Paramount's $81 billion takeover. The deal still faces regulatory reviews, including from the US Department of Justice. The outcome of these reviews will determine the merger's final approval.

item.title: $106 Billion Loan Reflects E.U.’s View That Peace in Ukraine Is Far Away
item.description: The European Union loaned Ukraine $106 billion for military spending. Unlike previous European assistance packages, this one is heavily weighted toward military spending, meant to put Ukraine on solid footing for a long fight.

item.title: US-Kuwaiti journalist held in Kuwait over social media posts acquitted, lawyers say
item.description: US-Kuwaiti journalist Ahmed Shihab-Eldin was acquitted in Kuwait over social media posts. He faced charges, including spreading false information, for re-posting images related to the Iran war. Shihab-Eldin's lawyers confirmed the acquittal.

item.title: Israel attacks kill journalist and target first responders
item.description: Israeli strikes in Lebanon killed a journalist and targeted first responders. A fragile ceasefire holds as Israeli strikes kill a journalist and target first responders in Lebanon. Israel attacks kill journalist and target first responders.
